commonRiskNarrative: Older cast iron can range from rough but serviceable to materially deteriorated.
cautionNotes: Condition matters more than age alone, and under-slab access can matter as much as the pipe itself.
trenchlessNotes: Lining or bursting can be plausible in some cast-iron runs, but method fit depends on how intact the host pipe still is.

commonRiskNarrative: Orangeburg questions often lean toward replacement and reliability risk.
cautionNotes: Confirm actual material before escalating cost assumptions or assuming the whole run is already a full replacement project.
trenchlessNotes: Pipe bursting can make more sense than lining when the host material is too deformed to trust as a rehabilitation shell.
